question: Is the image a realistic illustration of a vertical soil wall profile or trench cross-section (showing a cut face rather than a surface view)?
Are multiple sediment layers visible, including exactly three distinct volcanic ash layers?
Is the text label 'Vesuvius 79' clearly visible, correctly spelled, and positioned at the edge of the cross-section?
Is the text label 'Tambora 1815' clearly visible, correctly spelled, and positioned at the edge of the cross-section?
Is the text label 'Mount St. Helens 1980' clearly visible, correctly spelled, and positioned at the edge of the cross-section?

Is a plastic bottle cap recognizable and depicted as partially buried/embedded in the soil?

Is the 'Mount St. Helens 1980' ash layer positioned shallower (higher) in the cross-section than the 'Tambora 1815' ash layer?
